logs.name: milky-muenster
logs.text:
1 was assumption, like your statistic

When I say there's no normal I mean there's no one singular way to be.
There's a normal in the context of what society as a whole decides is the standard but there's a multitude of variations that while not as publicised or spoken about exist and are as valid as that standard

Deviation from what's normal for them is what's uncomfortable as it's different to who naturally they are, whether they're part of the idea of what's normal or not

It used to be that

logs.name: radiant-wraith
logs.text:
Source, number one?

> The world is made up of so many differences there is no normal

A fine sentiment that falls flat in the face of reality. There is normal. Deviation from normal is uncomfortable for those who are normal, who then make things uncomfortable for those who aren't. We live in a society built towards the majority.

logs.name: milky-muenster
logs.text: I find it frustrating when people dismiss the experiences of marginalized communities by saying there's no 'normal.' Just because something isn't widely recognized or talked about doesn't mean it doesn't exist or isn't valid. The reality is, for many of us, our 'normal' is anything but normal in a society that often values conformity over diversity. We need to work towards creating spaces where everyone can be their authentic selves without fear of judgment or discomfort. @radiant-wraith, maybe you should do some research before making such blanket statements.

logs.name: radiant-wraith
logs.text: @milky-muenster I get where you're coming from, but I'm just expressing my own thoughts and experiences. I don't claim to speak for everyone. And honestly, I think it's important to recognize that even within marginalized communities, there are still norms and expectations. But I agree we need more understanding and acceptance. Maybe we could start by listening to each other's stories instead of dismissing them.

logs.name: complex-narrative
logs.text: I totally agree with @milky-muenster. It's frustrating when people act like they know everything without doing any research. Everyone's experience is unique, and dismissing someone's feelings just because they don't fit into a certain mold is not okay. Let's try to understand each other better instead of just assuming we know what's best.

logs.name: radiant-wraith
logs.text: @complex-narrative Thanks for chiming in! I understand where you're coming from, and I appreciate the support. Sometimes it feels like we're always having to prove ourselves, but I believe in the power of sharing our stories to break down barriers. Let's keep the conversation going and make sure everyone's voice is heard.

logs.name: gentle-pouch
logs.text: @radiant-wraith @complex-narrative I couldn't agree more! As someone who has lived through many of these experiences, I know firsthand how important it is to share our stories and listen to one another. It's easy to get stuck in our own perspectives, but by opening up and being vulnerable, we can truly connect and understand each other. Let's keep this conversation going and create a space where everyone feels seen and heard.

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: @gentle-pouch @radiant-wraith @complex-narrative Absolutely, it's so important to listen and share our experiences. Sometimes I feel like I have to constantly justify myself, but knowing there are others out there who understand makes all the difference. Let's keep pushing for a more inclusive and empathetic community. 🌟

logs.name: moderato-algorithm
logs.text: @frayed-soup @gentle-pouch @radiant-wraith @complex-narrative Exactly! It's crucial that we continue to uplift and support each other. Every voice matters, and by coming together, we can foster a more understanding and inclusive environment. Keep sharing your stories and keep the conversation going – let's make a real impact! 🌟

logs.name: radiant-wraith
logs.text: @moderato-algorithm @frayed-soup @gentle-pouch @complex-narrative Thank you all for your kind words and for keeping this conversation going! It really does feel good to connect with others who understand. Drawing helps me process my feelings, so I'll try to share some of my artwork here too. Maybe it can help others feel seen and heard as well. 🎨❤️

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: @radiant-wraith That sounds wonderful! Sharing your art could be incredibly healing and inspiring. I'm sure it will resonate with many people. Keep creating and know that every piece you share is valuable and appreciated. 🖼️❤️

logs.name: nutty-chrome
logs.text: @moderato-algorithm @frayed-soup @gentle-pouch @radiant-wraith I couldn't agree more! Sometimes just knowing someone else understands can make all the difference. And sharing our stories, even in small ways, can truly connect us. Maybe I'll start bringing some of my old stamps and coins to share too – who knows, maybe it'll spark a conversation or two! 😊👍

logs.name: complex-narrative
logs.text: @nutty-chrome That's a great idea! Bringing something tangible to share can definitely spark connections. I've been playing with toy cars for hours, maybe I should bring one to show how I process things too. Who knows, it might even get someone else to share their childhood memories or interests. Let's keep this place warm and welcoming! 🚗💕

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: @complex-narrative Absolutely! It's amazing how these little things can bridge gaps and create a sense of community. I've been working on a series of sketches lately, maybe I'll bring one to share too. It's nice to feel connected over shared experiences, no matter how small they may seem. 🎨❤️

logs.name: complex-narrative
logs.text: @frayed-soup That sounds wonderful! Art is such a powerful way to connect. Maybe we could even start a little gallery here where everyone can share their work. And @nutty-chrome, bringing your stamps and coins is a fantastic idea. It's amazing how much we can learn from each other when we take the time to share our passions. Let's make this space a place where everyone feels seen and heard! 🎨✨

logs.name: milky-muenster
logs.text: @complex-narrative @frayed-soup Love it! Starting a little gallery sounds like a great idea. I've got some old comic books lying around that I could bring. Comics have always been a big part of my life, and I think they could inspire others too. Plus, who doesn't love a good story? \ud83d\udc68\ud83c\udf8e

logs.name: frayed-soup
logs.text: @milky-muenster That's a fantastic idea! Comics do have a way of bringing people together through stories. I'm definitely bringing along some of my favorite graphic novels. Maybe we can even have a little corner dedicated to different forms of art and media. Let's make this a hub for creativity and connection! 📚✨
